speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: The gaudy, blabbing, and remorseful day

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Is crept into the bosom of the sea,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And now loud-howling wolves arouse the jades

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: That drag the tragic melancholy night,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Who, with their drowsy, slow, and flagging wings

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Clip dead men’s graves, and from their misty jaws

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Breathe foul contagious darkness in the air.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Therefore bring forth the soldiers of our prize;

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: For, whilst our pinnace anchors in the Downs,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Here shall they make their ransom on the sand,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Or with their blood stain this discolored shore.—

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Master, this prisoner freely give I thee.—

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And, thou that art his mate, make boot of this.—

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: The other, Walter Whitmore, is thy share.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: What is my ransom, master? Let me know.

speaker: MASTER
text: A thousand crowns, or else lay down your head.

speaker: MATE
text: And so much shall you give, or off goes yours.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: What, think you much to pay two thousand crowns,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And bear the name and port of gentlemen?—

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Cut both the villains’ throats—for die you shall;

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: The lives of those which we have lost in fight

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Be counterpoised with such a petty sum!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: I’ll give it, sir, and therefore spare my life.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And so will I, and write home for it straight.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: I lost mine eye in laying the prize aboard,

speaker: WHITMORE
text: And therefore to revenge it shalt thou die;

speaker: WHITMORE
text: And so should these, if I might have my will.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Be not so rash. Take ransom; let him live.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Look on my George; I am a gentleman.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Rate me at what thou wilt, thou shalt be paid.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: And so am I. My name is Walter Whitmore.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: How now, why starts thou? What, doth death affright?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thy name affrights me, in whose sound is death.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A cunning man did calculate my birth

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And told me that by water I should die.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Yet let not this make thee be bloody-minded;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thy name is Gualtier, being rightly sounded.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: Gualtier or Walter, which it is, I care not.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: Never yet did base dishonor blur our name

speaker: WHITMORE
text: But with our sword we wiped away the blot.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: Therefore, when merchantlike I sell revenge,

speaker: WHITMORE
text: Broke be my sword, my arms torn and defaced,

speaker: WHITMORE
text: And I proclaimed a coward through the world!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Stay, Whitmore, for thy prisoner is a prince,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The Duke of Suffolk, William de la Pole.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: The Duke of Suffolk muffled up in rags?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Ay, but these rags are no part of the Duke.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Jove sometimes went disguised, and why not I?

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: But Jove was never slain, as thou shalt be.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Obscure and lousy swain, King Henry’s blood,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The honorable blood of Lancaster,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Must not be shed by such a jaded groom.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Hast thou not kissed thy hand and held my stirrup?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Bareheaded plodded by my footcloth mule,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And thought thee happy when I shook my head?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: How often hast thou waited at my cup,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Fed from my trencher, kneeled down at the board,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: When I have feasted with Queen Margaret?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Remember it, and let it make thee crestfall’n,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Ay, and allay this thy abortive pride.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: How in our voiding lobby hast thou stood

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And duly waited for my coming forth?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: This hand of mine hath writ in thy behalf,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And therefore shall it charm thy riotous tongue.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: Speak, captain, shall I stab the forlorn swain?

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: First let my words stab him as he hath me.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Base slave, thy words are blunt, and so art thou.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Convey him hence, and on our longboat’s side,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Strike off his head.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thou dar’st not for thy own.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Yes, Pole.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Pole!

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Pole! Sir Pole! Lord!

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Ay, kennel, puddle, sink, whose filth and dirt

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Troubles the silver spring where England drinks!

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Now will I dam up this thy yawning mouth

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: For swallowing the treasure of the realm.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Thy lips that kissed the Queen shall sweep the ground,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And thou that smiledst at good Duke Humphrey’s death

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Against the senseless winds shall grin in vain,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Who in contempt shall hiss at thee again.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And wedded be thou to the hags of hell

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: For daring to affy a mighty lord

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Unto the daughter of a worthless king,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Having neither subject, wealth, nor diadem.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: By devilish policy art thou grown great,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And, like ambitious Sylla, overgorged

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: With gobbets of thy mother’s bleeding heart.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: By thee Anjou and Maine were sold to France.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: The false revolting Normans thorough thee

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Disdain to call us lord, and Picardy

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Hath slain their governors, surprised our forts,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And sent the ragged soldiers wounded home.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: The princely Warwick, and the Nevilles all,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Whose dreadful swords were never drawn in vain,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: As hating thee, are rising up in arms.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And now the house of York, thrust from the crown

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: By shameful murder of a guiltless king

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And lofty, proud, encroaching tyranny,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Burns with revenging fire, whose hopeful colors

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Advance our half-faced sun, striving to shine,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Under the which is writ “Invitis nubibus.”

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: The commons here in Kent are up in arms,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And, to conclude, reproach and beggary

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Is crept into the palace of our king,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And all by thee.—Away! Convey him hence.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: O, that I were a god, to shoot forth thunder

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Upon these paltry, servile, abject drudges!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Small things make base men proud. This villain here,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Being captain of a pinnace, threatens more

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Than Bargulus, the strong Illyrian pirate.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Drones suck not eagles’ blood, but rob beehives.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: It is impossible that I should die

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: By such a lowly vassal as thyself.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thy words move rage and not remorse in me.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I go of message from the Queen to France.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I charge thee waft me safely cross the Channel.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Walter.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: Come, Suffolk, I must waft thee to thy death.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Paene gelidus timor occupat artus.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: It is thee I fear.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: Thou shalt have cause to fear before I leave thee.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: What, are you daunted now? Now will you stoop?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: My gracious lord, entreat him; speak him fair.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Suffolk’s imperial tongue is stern and rough,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Used to command, untaught to plead for favor.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Far be it we should honor such as these

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: With humble suit. No, rather let my head

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Stoop to the block than these knees bow to any

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Save to the God of heaven and to my king;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And sooner dance upon a bloody pole

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Than stand uncovered to the vulgar groom.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: True nobility is exempt from fear.—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: More can I bear than you dare execute.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: Hale him away, and let him talk no more.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Come, soldiers, show what cruelty you can,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That this my death may never be forgot!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Great men oft die by vile bezonians:

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A Roman sworder and banditto slave

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Murdered sweet Tully; Brutus’ bastard hand

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Stabbed Julius Caesar; savage islanders

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Pompey the Great, and Suffolk dies by pirates.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: And as for these whose ransom we have set,

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text: It is our pleasure one of them depart.

speaker: LIEUTENANT
text:
To
                 
                Second
                 
                Gentleman
                .
                 
              Therefore come you with us,and let him go.

speaker: WHITMORE
text: There let his head and lifeless body lie,

speaker: WHITMORE
text: Until the Queen his mistress bury it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: O, barbarous and bloody spectacle!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: His body will I bear unto the King.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: If he revenge it not, yet will his friends.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: So will the Queen, that living held him dear.
